title: Coleridge, Southey And Wordsworth.
content:
England had triplets at a birth,
            Coleridge, Southey and Wordsworth,
            And these three are widely famed,
            And the "Lake Poets" they were named.
            With joy they did pursue their themes,
            'Mong England's lakes and hills and streams,
            From there with gladness they could view
            The distant Scottish mountains blue.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James McIntyre, titled "Coleridge, Southey And Wordsworth.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
